reasoning: The historical base rate of white-collar employment share dropping by 2 full percentage points (from ~62% to under 60%) in a few years is extremely low, especially since this share has trended upward for decades. The Metaculus community probability on June 12, 2025 was 7%, strongly implying that this threshold was not breached in any quarter from Q2 2023 through Q1 2025. With only Q2 (nearly concluded), Q3, and Q4 of 2025 remaining, an abrupt >2 percentage point decline in white-collar employment share over a 6-month period would require massive, rapid, asymmetric job losses for white-collar workers (e.g., millions of jobs) that is highly incongruent with current labor market trends. I forecast 6%, maintaining closely to the informed community baseline.
